headline: Klang Valley Congestion Costs Economy RM12 Billion Yearly
context: Transport Ministry study found KL traffic congestion costs RM12 billion annually in lost productivity and fuel waste.

cards.big: KL congestion costs economy RM12 billion annually in lost productivity
cards.sub: Average commuter loses 45 minutes daily to congestion up from 35 in 2020

cards.big: Vehicle registration grew 4.5% annually reaching 7.8 million in 2025
cards.sub: Road capacity expanded only 1.2% annually creating widening gap

cards.big: Morning peak speeds on expressways averaged 22km/h in 2025
cards.sub: Pre-pandemic average was 32km/h indicating permanent worsening

cards.big: Congestion pricing for KL proposed in 2024 shelved after public backlash
cards.sub: Singapore ERP reduces CBD traffic 24% but Malaysian political will lacking

cards.big: Congestion costs test whether pricing mechanisms can overcome political resistance
RM12 billion yearly congestion cost. Road pricing would fund the transit that reduces the congestion.
